frameworks.name: holistic matching model
frameworks.one_line: perceptual inference and matching jointly operate across representational hierarchy

frameworks.name: perceptual matching interpretation
frameworks.one_line: method-of-adjustment bias reflects relative perceptual difference between probe and test

core_question: Does the method of adjustment yield unbiased matches when test and probe are identical, as the perceptual matching interpretation predicts?
key_assumptions: matching identical stimuli should cancel perceptual distortions
broadband orientation-filtered noise drives orientation estimation
matches reflect inference plus a matching process across hierarchy levels
manipulations: noise level of test and probe (low vs high)
key_findings: robust repulsive biases away from cardinal orientations in same-noise (identical) conditions
cross-noise biases did not invert when test and probe were switched
interpretations: matches cannot be purely perceptual since identical stimuli still yield biases
a holistic matching model jointly fits present and prior method-of-adjustment data
